verification_gate: Red tests prove JSONL, JSON, Nuon, native record/list/table, binary raw linkage, length/digest rejection, idempotent object assignment, MessagePack child ordering, typed receipts, and redb/PostgreSQL metadata parity.

gate_result.red_first: contracts committed red at nu_plugin e3c933d (stubs fail closed: 7/10 red with the 3 passes being all-rejection assertions), implementation green at cd26f50
gate_result.nuon_coverage: Nuon-origin data reaches the plugin as native Nu records/lists over the MessagePack protocol (open file.nuon | codedb ingest-envelope); the record and list paths are proven live e2e
gate_result.rejection_evidence: per-frame sha256 recomputed against decoded base64 payloads; byte_length must equal decoded length; per-stream offsets contiguous from zero; sequences strictly monotonic; completion totals and frame_count must agree; truncated JSONL (no execution_complete) rejected; live e2e corrupts every frame digest and asserts nonzero exit
gate_result.idempotency_evidence: canonical raw_object_id = sha256 of the exact reassembled stream bytes; re-ingest yields identical ids with dedup marked; pre-assigned canonical ids pass only when they match the recomputed address; identical bytes ingested as typed source blobs dedup against raw objects — one content-addressed identity space, no raw/typed split
gate_result.protocol_evidence: live e2e drives the real nu_plugin_codedb child over MessagePack (--plugin-config/--plugins) for record, re-ingest, and list paths; canonical ids are byte-identical across JSON-file, JSONL-file, record-protocol, and list-protocol inputs from separate real rtk_nu executions; traces preserved at logs/raw-envelope-protocol/traces.json
gate_result.receipt_evidence: codedb.raw-ingest-receipt.v0 carries idempotency_key, per-stream raw_object rows (id, byte_length, frame_count, deduplicated), and truthful exit metadata including signal death (code=null/signal preserved)
gate_result.parity_evidence: codedb_raw_objects lands identical rows under identical canonical ids; pg-integration parity test compares parsed metadata values row-for-row against list_raw_objects on a real PostgreSQL 17.10 service (2x flush proves ON CONFLICT idempotency)
gate_result.product_execution_evidence: live e2e executes the profile-pinned rtk_nu against a two-stream child (sh -c printf interleave), ingests the resulting envelopes through all four input paths, and proves stdout raw_object_id sha256:b94d27b9… (the canonical digest of 'hello world') identical everywhere

proof_summary: ARCHBP-041 is complete: the typed codedb ingest-envelope command now accepts rtk_nu raw envelopes in every rendering — JSON aggregate, JSONL event stream, JSON event array, and native Nu record/list over the real MessagePack child-plugin protocol — validates them fail-closed (per-frame digest and length recomputation, contiguous offsets, strictly monotonic sequences, completion agreement, truncated-stream rejection), reassembles byte-exact streams, and assigns canonical content-addressed raw_object_id values idempotently in the same identity space as typed source bytes. Typed receipts echo idempotency keys and truthful exit metadata; raw-report reads back stream, frame, digest, and idempotency metadata; and redb/PostgreSQL metadata parity is proven row-for-row against a real PostgreSQL 17.10 service. Red-first at nu_plugin e3c933d, green at cd26f50 (PR FlexNetOS/nu_plugin#44, stacked on #43), workspace 302/0, live e2e traces preserved.
